V-M-074.scope: Responsive adaptation verification for phone, tablet, and desktop layouts across public, auth, user-cabinet, and admin Matrix surfaces.
V-M-074.verification-intent: Phase-61 must prove that the premium Matrix UI is intentionally designed for mobile and tablet use, not merely tolerated by desktop CSS.

commands.command: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/verification-index.xml docs/verification/V-M-074.xml docs/modules/M-074.xml docs/plans/Phase-61.xml
git diff --name-only -- backend deploy docker-compose.yml install.sh mtproto-runtime official-mtproxy nginx | test -z "$(cat)"

viewport-matrix.phone-1: 320x568 proves smallest supported phone does not clip auth/public CTAs or cabinet actions.
viewport-matrix.phone-2: 360x740 proves compact Android layout, bottom navigation spacing, forms, and MTProto/VPN cards.
viewport-matrix.phone-3: 375x667 proves legacy iPhone-height constraints without hidden primary controls.
viewport-matrix.phone-4: 390x844 proves common iPhone layout, safe-area padding, calendar/subscription and config panels.
viewport-matrix.phone-5: 430x932 proves large phone density without oversized hero, cards, or chart frames.
viewport-matrix.tablet-1: 768x1024 proves portrait tablet layout has intentional two-column or bounded single-column behavior.
viewport-matrix.tablet-2: 820x1180 proves iPad-like portrait layout preserves readable public, cabinet, and admin surfaces.
viewport-matrix.tablet-3: 1024x768 proves landscape tablet does not become cramped desktop or stretched phone layout.
viewport-matrix.tablet-4: 1024x1366 proves tall tablet keeps navigation, charts, lists, and forms within predictable bounds.
viewport-matrix.desktop-1: 1280x720 proves short desktop remains compact and primary content is not pushed below useful view.
viewport-matrix.desktop-2: 1440x900 proves premium desktop regression remains stable after mobile/tablet changes.
viewport-matrix.reduced-motion-1: 390x844 with reduced motion proves Matrix and route transitions remain readable.
viewport-matrix.reduced-motion-2: 768x1024 with reduced motion proves tablet UI remains usable without animated cues.

route-matrix.auth-limitation-policy: If local auth fixtures cannot enter protected routes, the smoke must produce static-route proof and record the remaining live screenshot gap instead of claiming false coverage.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: All planned phone, tablet, desktop, and reduced-motion viewports render without document-level or route-level horizontal scroll.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: Public/auth surfaces keep the first-screen brand signal, CTA, email/password forms, password hint, and recovery paths visible and non-overlapping.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: User cabinet keeps VPN config, MTProto proxy links, subscription countdown/calendar, referrals, and settings actions reachable on phone and tablet.
success-scenarios.scenario-4: Admin cockpit keeps dashboard metrics, users/devices lists, MTProto analytics, abuse alerts, servers, plans, and analytics charts bounded and readable.
success-scenarios.scenario-5: Inputs, icons, placeholders, labels, badges, chart legends, copy buttons, and action buttons do not overlap or resize their containers unexpectedly.
success-scenarios.scenario-6: Touch targets for primary navigation and actions are at least 44px in the planned phone/tablet breakpoints unless the element is non-interactive text.
success-scenarios.scenario-7: Mobile bottom navigation, sticky headers, fixed backgrounds, Matrix canvas, and scroll containers do not cover primary content or block pointer/scroll gestures.
success-scenarios.scenario-8: Tablet layouts show deliberate density and hierarchy: no lazy stretched phone layout, no cramped desktop table, and no nested-card visual bulk.
success-scenarios.scenario-9: Reduced-motion mode disables or softens motion while preserving status feedback, Matrix atmosphere, route readability, and focus visibility.
success-scenarios.scenario-10: Phase-60 premium QA smoke remains green after responsive edits, proving no regression in visual policy, assets, motion, accessibility, or bundle budgets.

failure-scenarios.failure-1: Any viewport hides or clips registration, login, password reset, VPN config, MTProto link, subscription, admin health, or admin action paths.
failure-scenarios.failure-2: Any document/body/route container produces horizontal scroll beyond a 2px measurement tolerance.
failure-scenarios.failure-3: Any icon overlaps placeholder text, field value, label, badge, button text, or chart/table content.
failure-scenarios.failure-4: Any phone/tablet route requires unreasonable vertical scanning because repeated cards, headings, or decorative surfaces dominate useful controls.
failure-scenarios.failure-5: Any admin table/list grows without internal bounding or loses search/filter/action reachability on phones or tablets.
failure-scenarios.failure-6: Any responsive fix changes backend APIs, deploy/install scripts, docker-compose, nginx, mtproto-runtime, official-mtproxy, or live operational behavior.
failure-scenarios.failure-7: Any smoke output exposes secrets, tokens, real emails, raw config payloads, WireGuard keys, MTProto secrets, payment IDs, or operator infrastructure credentials.

telemetry-requirements.allowed: route path, viewport size, app surface name, selector label, measured width/height, horizontal-scroll delta, screenshot artifact path, bundle size, and anonymized failure code.
telemetry-requirements.forbidden: passwords, access tokens, refresh tokens, API keys, Resend/YooKassa secrets, WireGuard private keys, MTProto secrets, full proxy links, real emails, raw user IPs, payment identifiers, and server access credentials.
telemetry-requirements.retention: Local screenshot and smoke artifacts may be stored under /tmp/krotpn-phase61-* during verification and must not be committed.

performance-budgets.frontend-js: Built user frontend JavaScript should stay within the Phase-60 budget unless a justified delta is recorded.
performance-budgets.frontend-css: Built user frontend CSS should stay within the Phase-60 budget unless a justified delta is recorded.
performance-budgets.admin-js: Built admin frontend JavaScript should stay within the Phase-60 budget unless a justified delta is recorded.
performance-budgets.admin-css: Built admin frontend CSS should stay within the Phase-60 budget unless a justified delta is recorded.
performance-budgets.asset-policy: No new public visual asset may exceed 512KB without explicit documentation, and no single frontend asset may exceed 1.5MB.

phase-gates.gate-1: V-M-074 cannot move to pass-local until user/admin builds, Phase-61 smoke, Phase-60 regression smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected surface guard pass.
phase-gates.gate-2: V-M-074 cannot move to pass-local if any protected-route screenshots are unavailable without a truthful auth-fixture/live-gap note.
phase-gates.gate-3: Phase-61 execution must not modify deploy/install scripts or backend/runtime services; any required operational change must become a separate confirmed phase.
phase-gates.gate-4: Known live-only evidence must be documented in M-074 and V-M-074 rather than hidden behind generic PASS wording.

autonomous-execution-safety.safety-1: Edits are limited to frontend, frontend-admin, Phase-61 smoke, and MyGRACE documentation unless the operator approves a new phase.
autonomous-execution-safety.safety-2: Use deterministic smoke assertions before screenshot review; screenshots are evidence, not the only gate.
autonomous-execution-safety.safety-3: Prefer compact responsive CSS and existing components over adding new visual systems or business workflows.
autonomous-execution-safety.safety-4: Do not commit generated screenshots, dist artifacts, local secrets, or temporary Playwright state.
